skill.name: reactome-skill
skill.description: Query the Reactome REST API for pathway content and enrichment analyses; use when you need curated pathway data, reaction details, or overrepresentation results for a gene list.
skill.summary: Query Reactome pathways for gene enrichment and pathway content analysis via the official REST API.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluation of 44 static findings confirms all are false positives. The HIGH severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are caused by Reactome stable identifiers (R-HSA-xxxxx format) matching hash patterns in the scanner. All 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code block examples in documentation files, not actual execution. Network and external_commands usage is legitimate and documented for the Reactome REST API. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External command execution via Python script
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI to run python scripts/reactome_tool.py as a shell command. This is expected behavior for the skill and the script only makes authorized calls to the Reactome REST API.

low_findings.title: Network calls to external API
low_findings.description: The Python script makes HTTP requests to reactome.org, a legitimate academic bioinformatics database. All URLs are hardcoded and transparent.

content.user_title: Explore Reactome pathways for gene enrichment analysis
content.value_statement: Researchers need a fast way to query curated biological pathway data from the Reactome database. This skill provides direct access to the Reactome REST API for pathway content retrieval and gene overrepresentation analysis through a simple command-line interface.
actual_capabilities: Query Reactome pathway content by stable identifier (e.g., R-HSA-69278)
Run gene overrepresentation (enrichment) analysis against Reactome pathways
Retrieve curated reaction details and participating molecules for a given pathway
Access pathway hierarchy to navigate curated pathway structures
Submit comma-separated gene lists for automated enrichment analysis
limitations: Requires internet access to query the Reactome REST API
Only supports human pathways by default; species projection requires additional parameters
Does not support batch queries or paginated results from the API
Analysis results depend on Reactome service availability and response times

use_cases.title: Pathway enrichment for gene lists
use_cases.description: A researcher has a list of differentially expressed genes from an RNA-seq experiment and wants to identify enriched biological pathways in the Reactome database.

use_cases.title: Pathway content lookup for teaching
use_cases.description: An educator needs to retrieve details about specific Reactome pathways to prepare lecture materials on cellular signaling and metabolic pathways.

use_cases.title: Systems biology workflow integration
use_cases.description: A data scientist building a multi-step analysis pipeline needs programmatic access to Reactome for pathway-level interpretation of gene sets.

prompt_templates.title: Pathway content query
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the pathway details for the Reactome ID R-HSA-69278.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quickly look up a specific pathway structure and its participating molecules.

prompt_templates.title: Gene enrichment analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run overrepresentation analysis for these genes: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, MYC, KRAS.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify which Reactome pathways are statistically enriched in a given gene list.

prompt_templates.title: Custom gene list analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the following gene identifiers for pathway enrichment: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR.
prompt_templates.scenario: Submit a custom comma-separated gene list for enrichment analysis against Reactome pathways.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step pathway research
prompt_templates.prompt: First query the pathway content for R-HSA-69278 to understand its structure, then analyze the genes TP53, BRCA1, EGFR for overrepresentation to find related pathways.
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine content lookup and enrichment analysis in a single research workflow.

output_examples.input: Query pathway details for Reactome ID R-HSA-69278.
output_examples.output: The pathway Cell Cycle (R-HSA-69278) contains 15 sub-pathways including G1/S transition, G2/M transition, and mitotic cell cycle checkpoints. Each sub-pathway lists participating proteins, complexes, and reactions with literature references.

output_examples.input: Analyze the genes TP53, BRCA1, EGFR for pathway enrichment.
output_examples.output: The analysis found 8 significantly enriched pathways with p-value below 0.05. The top 3 results are DNA Damage Response (p=0.0001), Cell Cycle Regulation (p=0.0003), and Signaling by EGFR (p=0.002). Each pathway entry includes the specific genes involved and the statistical significance score.

The pathway Cell Cycle Checkpoints (R-HSA-1640170) is a sub-pathway of Cell Cycle (R-HSA-69278). It contains 3 child pathways: G1/S DNA Damage Checkpoints, G2/M Checkpoints, and Mitotic Spindle Checkpoint. Each child pathway has associated proteins and molecular interactions.

best_practices: Use official Reactome stable identifiers in R-HSA-xxxxx format when querying pathway content for reliable results.
Provide gene identifiers in standard HGNC gene symbol format for best compatibility with the Reactome analysis service.
Start with a small gene list of 10 to 50 genes for enrichment analysis to validate the workflow before scaling up.
anti_patterns: Do not use non-standard gene identifiers or ambiguous names; this will cause the enrichment analysis to return errors or empty results.
Do not rely on the skill for real-time clinical decisions; Reactome data is curated for research purposes and not intended for direct clinical use.
Do not submit patient-level genomic data without understanding Reactome data handling policies and privacy implications.

faq.question: What is Reactome?
faq.answer: Reactome is an open-source, curated database of biological pathways maintained by expert biologists and bioinformaticians.

faq.question: How do I format gene identifiers for enrichment analysis?
faq.answer: Use standard HGNC gene symbols separated by commas, for example: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR.

faq.question: Does this skill need an internet connection?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill requires internet access to communicate with the Reactome REST API servers for all queries.

faq.question: What is a Reactome stable identifier?
faq.answer: A Reactome stable identifier is a unique ID in the format R-HSA-xxxxx, for example R-HSA-69278 for the Cell Cycle pathway.

faq.question: Can I analyze non-human species pathways?
faq.answer: The skill can be used with Reactome species projection features, but non-human analysis requires specifying the appropriate species parameters.

faq.question: What kind of results does the enrichment analysis return?
faq.answer: It returns a list of enriched pathways with statistical significance values and the specific genes that map to each pathway.
